functional.text: System accepts webhook URL registration via POST /webhooks with URL and event list
All webhook payloads include an HMAC-SHA256 signature in the X-Signature-256 header
Failed deliveries retry 3 times with exponential backoff (10s, 60s, 300s)
Users can list, update, and delete their registered webhooks via REST endpoints

non_functional.text: Webhook delivery latency P95 < 5 seconds from event trigger to first HTTP request sent
Webhook processing must not block the main event pipeline — use async queue
Signature verification must use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks

edge_cases.scenario: Target URL returns 5xx on all 3 retries
edge_cases.behavior: Mark webhook as failing, notify user via email, stop retrying until manually re-enabled

edge_cases.scenario: Target URL responds but takes > 30 seconds
edge_cases.behavior: Timeout after 30s, count as failed delivery, trigger retry

edge_cases.scenario: User deletes webhook while deliveries are queued
edge_cases.behavior: Cancel pending jobs for that webhook, do not deliver

fields.name: events
fields.description: List of event types to subscribe to

errors.meaning: Invalid URL format or empty events list
URL already registered for this user

clarifications.question: What happens after all 3 retries fail?
clarifications.answer: Mark the webhook as failing and send the user an email. Don't disable it permanently — let them fix the URL and re-enable.
